reasoning: The base rate of reconciliation for Trump's feuds is relatively high if the other party capitulates (e.g., JD Vance, Ted Cruz). The recent feud between Musk and Trump in early June 2025 escalated significantly, causing both parties to make extreme statements. However, as of June 11, Musk capitulated by deleting his most inflammatory tweets and expressing regret. Trump has publicly stated he holds "no hard feelings." Given Musk's structural dependence on government contracts (SpaceX) and policy (Tesla), he has strong incentives to repair the relationship. Six months is enough time for tensions to cool and for a transactional relationship to resume. A Manifold market was at 0.43 shortly after this news broke, which likely reflected the initial de-escalation. Given no major new flare-ups since mid-June, I estimate the probability of them being on subjectively "good terms" by the end of 2025 to be around 45%—slightly above the June 12 market as the de-escalation holds, but acknowledging the risk of future volatility due to their personalities.
